_comment: Migrations the prod DB ledger (workers_schema_version) reports as NOT recorded, that are KNOWN and accepted — so the predeploy migration gate does not cry-wolf on them. The gate blocks only on missing migrations NOT in this list (genuine new deploy-ahead-of-schema drift), plus the placeholder/unreachable DATABASE_URL footguns. Burn this list down: when a 'staged_absent' entry is applied to prod, remove it; when an 'applied_unrecorded' entry's version is back-filled into the ledger, remove it.
audit_method: compared src/workers/db/migrations vs prod workers_schema_version + to_regclass() table-existence probes on prod flat-truth-23350426
reconciled: 2026-07-19: prod fully reconciled — applied 040/063/064/065 (recorded their versions + created mcp_access_log/llm_usage_log/idempotency_keys, which HEAD code required) and back-filled the 037/038 ledger rows (their tables pre-existed; those migrations predate the version-recording convention). verify-prod-migrations now PASS (0 missing, exit 0). Baseline emptied — any future gate finding is genuine new drift.
